title: Mobile navigation drawer redesign
items: Made the mobile drawer compact with grouped navigation rows instead of oversized vertical spacing.
Changed the active menu item into a consistent selected row rather than a large card.
Added menu/account hierarchy labels, improved the close button visibility and kept the account controls anchored to the bottom.

title: Mobile drawer spacing fix
items: Grouped mobile menu links with fluid spacing.
Prevented active drawer items from expanding into oversized cards.
Kept the account block anchored at the bottom of the drawer.

title: Fluid mobile drawer spacing
items: Reworked the mobile navigation drawer with responsive spacing instead of device-specific fixed gaps.
Kept menu links compact on tall phones while preserving comfortable touch targets on smaller screens.
Prevented the drawer overlay from compressing or shifting dashboard content while open.

title: Moderation bypass and mobile polish
items: Added moderation bypass access management with default bot owner and Manage Server users.
Added username search/autocomplete for AI image access and moderation bypass user pickers.
Kept Discord ID entry for manual user additions.
Tightened the mobile hamburger button and aligned dashboard switches.

changes.text: Connected Welcome/Goodbye, Leveling, Logs and Moderation dashboard forms to protected bot API endpoints instead of local preview storage only.
Level reward roles now load real guild roles, save reward mappings through the API and remove the old placeholder Add Role state.
Logs and moderation tabs now use API-backed settings with channel dropdowns and real bot-side event handlers where supported.
Compressed the mobile navbar, hamburger and right-side drawer so navigation takes less vertical space while keeping touch targets usable.
Reduced mobile dashboard header, badges, tabs, cards and form spacing to show more server settings above the fold.
Tightened Welcome/Goodbye message controls, smaller textareas and channel dropdown styling while keeping welcome and goodbye channels separate.
Split the server Welcome Messages tab into separate welcome and goodbye settings with independent enable toggles, channel dropdowns and message fields.
The Discord preview now shows both join and leave messages with their selected channels so they are no longer treated as one shared setting.
Reduced the mobile navigation drawer size with tighter spacing and a right-side compact layout for easier one-handed use.
Added a real dashboard channel API fallback so server channel dropdowns can load from the bot API or directly from Discord with DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN.
Server dashboard responses now include channel source metadata and clearer channel API errors for unavailable channel lists.
Channel lookup remains protected by the existing dashboard access guard, while demo mode continues using isolated fake channels only.
Server dashboard channel settings now use Discord channel dropdowns instead of manual text inputs.
Welcome, leveling, logs and moderation tabs share the same channel selector component with demo channels in preview mode.
Dashboard server responses can now include real text channels from the bot API while demo mode stays fake-data only and read-only.
Demo routes now render a demo-specific loading state immediately instead of the shared Checking login placeholder.
The shared navbar detects /demo pages before auth runs, keeping QA monitors from treating demo preview pages as login-gated.
Demo pages still bypass real Discord auth only with fake, read-only data and normal protected dashboard routes remain login-only.
Removed leftover Discord login prompts from demo dashboard and demo settings so QA no longer flags demo pages as protected routes.
Demo read-only warnings now avoid auth-required wording while still making it clear that real changes are disabled.
Demo mobile drawer now offers Exit Demo instead of Login with Discord, keeping the preview context fake-data only.
Restored the npm monitor script used by the GitHub Actions website monitor workflow.
Added a Playwright-based monitor runner for homepage, documentation, changelog, demo dashboard and demo settings routes.
Monitor screenshots are saved to monitor-artifacts so failed workflow runs can upload QA evidence.
Added the missing public /demo/dashboard route and kept /demo as a public demo dashboard entry point.
Demo settings now bypasses Discord login safely with fake demo data and keeps the Demo Mode read-only banner visible.
Demo pages are served from a demo-only HTML entry so login-required fallback content is not detected on public demo routes.
Demo dashboard, demo settings and demo server pages keep the same isolated demo context, fake user and read-only behavior.
Added a public /demo route for previewing protected dashboard and settings pages without Discord login.
Demo mode now uses isolated fake user, server, bot stats, leveling and settings data from a dedicated demo data module.
Demo mode is read-only and does not call private dashboard APIs or allow server/account modifications.
Demo pages show a clear Demo Mode banner and can be used to test breadcrumbs, navigation, mobile menu, owner/user view and light/dark styling.
Removed the duplicate Settings entry from the authenticated mobile menu while keeping Settings available in the main menu.
Hid Dashboard navigation for guests and kept Settings available for both logged-in and logged-out visitors.
Settings now saves light/dark mode and language locally for this browser without requiring Discord login.
Hid Owner/User dashboard view controls from guests and kept them only for authenticated users.
Added the shared website footer to dashboard/settings routes for consistent page layout and credits.
Fixed the shared mobile menu disappearing after login/session initialization on every page.
Refactored the website to use shared layout components for the global navbar, mobile menu, theme loading and navigation items.
Unified the mobile menu across home, dashboard, documentation, changelog and settings.
Applied light/dark theme before page render to reduce dark-mode flashes during reloads.
Expanded light mode coverage for homepage, documentation, changelog, dashboard cards, status messages and menu states.
Fixed the mobile dashboard drawer so the background blurs while the menu remains sharp.
Restored dashboard breadcrumbs on mobile and desktop dashboard pages.
Added a Settings page for Owner/User view, theme preferences and future language selection.
Reorganized dashboard navigation into Dashboard, Resources and Account sections.
Added bot stats diagnostics to make Railway BOT_API_URL issues easier to identify.
Improved the mobile dashboard drawer so it closes cleanly and blocks background scrolling.
Compacted AI Image Access rows and improved mobile spacing.
Added log status pills and a starter level reward table preview.
Rebuilt the dashboard as a full app-style UI with a desktop sidebar, top bar, cleaner cards and stable mobile navigation.
Restored the Owner View/User View toggle and kept it inside the dashboard experience.
Reworked server tabs to share the same layout foundation across overview, welcome, leveling, logs, AI access and moderation.
Cleaned the Welcome Messages preview so it no longer renders oversized chat-log sections on mobile.
Reworked server settings pages toward the app-style dashboard layout with a desktop sidebar and cleaner settings workspace.
Restored the Owner View/User Preview dashboard toggle and preserved owner access while keeping normal user filtering.
Rebuilt Welcome Messages with a richer customization layout and Discord-style live preview.
Polished AI Image Access so default-access users and manually allowed users are shown as separate sections with user counts.
Added a confirmation modal before removing a manually allowed user.
Disabled Remove buttons now clearly show that bot owner and Manage Server access cannot be removed from the dashboard.
AI Image Access now separates default-access users from manually added users.
Users with Manage Server permission and the bot owner show with disabled Remove buttons because their access comes from permissions.
Polished server feature pages on mobile with a more compact server header and cleaner one-column settings layout.
Adjusted AI image access form and allowed-user cards so they fit better on phone screens.
Reduced duplicate Discord permission checks on dashboard server feature pages to prevent rate limit errors.
Improved AI image access loading so repeated refreshes share one request and show clearer errors.
Added skeleton pages for Welcome messages, Logs, AI image access and Moderation tools.
Expanded server dashboard tabs so every server feature section now opens its own dashboard page.
Added consistent coming soon previews and disabled save actions across all server setting pages.
Added the first server feature page for Leveling settings.
Added server dashboard section tabs for Overview, Leveling, Welcome messages, Logs, AI image access and Moderation tools.
Linked server tool cards to their related dashboard sections.
Made server overview headers match the blockless dashboard page layout.
Standardized page header spacing across dashboard, documentation and changelog pages.
Simplified the dashboard welcome area into a blockless hero with only the greeting and supporting text.
Removed extra dashboard hero buttons and summary stats so the server management sections appear sooner.
Fixed mobile dashboard server sections so installed and available server cards stack vertically instead of squeezing side by side.
Improved server row spacing, text truncation and action button alignment on small screens.
Consolidated website styles into a single stylesheet to reduce render-blocking requests.
Lazy-loaded page-specific JavaScript so pages only load the modules they need.
Added short caching for bot stats, commands and dashboard server data to improve perceived responsiveness.
Reduced heavy blur effects on mobile for smoother navigation and interactions.
Reworked the dashboard welcome card to match the homepage hero style with summary stats and primary actions.
Removed the separate quick actions card and moved the most important links into the dashboard hero.
Polished server overview pages with breadcrumb navigation, server status pills and clearer coming soon module cards.
Tightened dashboard server card spacing and reduced oversized mobile gaps.
Prevented server rows from turning into a large blue highlighted block when tapped on mobile.
Aligned dashboard server row action buttons and made server rows feel cleaner on mobile.
Removed duplicate Dashboard links from the account menu after moving Dashboard into the main navigation.
Improved server dashboard empty states and server overview coming soon cards.
Added basic server overview pages from the dashboard server list.
Server pages now show name, icon, server ID, member count, permission status and customization coming soon sections.
Updated the dashboard server rows so installed servers open their own overview page while available servers keep invite links.
Added cleaner spacing between dashboard welcome action buttons.
Polished server list row hover states and dashboard action layout.
Corrected dashboard server filtering so only manageable shared servers appear under Servers with Meowz.
Removed connected account and bot stat cards from the dashboard overview.
Stored Discord guild permissions from OAuth so Available Servers can show servers where the user can add Meowz.
Reworked the dashboard into a server-focused control panel.
Added Servers with Meowz and Available Servers sections for future server management.
Replaced profile-heavy dashboard cards with connected account, live stats and quick actions.
Split the website into dedicated Home, Dashboard, Documentation and Changelog routes.
Cleaned up navigation links so each major section now has its own URL.
Prepared the website structure for the next dashboard and settings features.
Added the first authenticated dashboard section with a personalized welcome and Discord account card.
Kept the Meowz brand fixed in the navbar while showing the logged-in user avatar and name on the right.
Cleaned up the mobile authenticated menu with a simple account header, Settings and Logout.
Kept the Meowz logo and brand visible after login instead of replacing it with the user avatar.
Reworked the authenticated navbar to show the user avatar and name beside a compact Settings and Logout dropdown.
Reworked the authenticated navbar so the user avatar replaces the brand area after login.
Changed the logged-in navigation actions into a cleaner Profile dropdown with Logout.
Added Discord OAuth login and logout routes.
Added /api/me so the website can display the logged-in Discord user.
Replaced the placeholder login button with a real Discord login state in the navbar.
Reduced the desktop hero height so the feature section appears sooner.
Polished the desktop homepage spacing, buttons, navbar and live stats card alignment.
Added cleaner hover styling and accent details to the feature cards.
Centered the mobile pagination controls.
Aligned changelog status badges and update text more cleanly on mobile.
Redesigned command documentation with a split-view layout on desktop and a bottom sheet on mobile.
Added shareable command hashes and copy buttons for command usage examples.
Renamed the commands navigation to Documentation to better match the new reference layout.
Replaced the browser page prompt with a custom page picker modal.
Polished mobile command rows and details so command names and descriptions stay readable.
Made command pagination responsive with Previous, Next and smart page jumping.
Improved the mobile commands table so long command names no longer overlap descriptions.
Rebuilt the mobile navigation with a hamburger menu.
Changed the commands area into a paginated table that renders 10 commands per page.
Added skeleton loading states for live stats, commands and changelog content.
Polished command details, footer styling and Discord preview metadata.
Added interactive command documentation.
Updated command cards with expandable details.
Added the public changelog section.
Updated the homepage navigation for the changelog.
Added the live commands page with search and category filtering.
Added public, admin and owner visibility badges for commands.
Connected the website to the bot API for live stats.
Added bot name, server count, user count, ping and uptime.
